title: Which Eva series Dvd should I be buying
display_title: Which Eva series Dvd should I be buying
wikitext:
[]: Which Eva series Dvd should I be buying?             Quote Post by NightEva » Sun May 29, 2016 6:31 pm Hey everyone ! I'm new to this forum and one question that I've been having is which Eva Dvd should I get, I really want to have the original series with me a long with EoE but when I searched for it online to order there were a bunch of different versions and box sets with prices ranging from 20 to like 200. I just want the original dub and director's cut, what would you guys recommend to buy maybe recommend something off amazon?

[]: Re: Which Eva series Dvd should I be buying?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon May 30, 2016 2:13 am First, let's get EoE out of the way. It was licensed in the US by a different company from the series, and so has never appeared in a single box with it here. Its US mastering was lousy, and has never been redone. For now we just have to suck it up. Speaking only of US (and UK) releases of the series, the "Perfect Collection" is the early one, based on the original video masters (well, actually the videodisc version, I think). It does not contain the DC episodes, but these can be acquired in less-common matching separate DVDs to replace the last two of the set. Its video quality is generally inferior. The remastering (called "Renewal" in Japan", but not in the US) is the basis of the "Platinum Collection" and all later ones are versions of it (tin box, Holiday edition, thinpaks). They are essentially equivalent, though there are slight variations in video bit rate - the sets with less disks have a higher bit rate and dump the extras). Unfortunately, as they've been out of print for so long, there are many bootlegs for sale, and some are not so easy to distinguish from the real thing. There are a number of threads in this forum discussing how to tell, and including example images - a forum search for "bootleg" and "platinum" will find them. Finally, note that in Japan there was a new BD release last year. No arrangements to license this in the US for release with with the English dub and subs are yet known of, nor whether such a licence would at last include the films in the same set (the companies that had the previous separate licences no longer exist in the same forms, and those licences are believed to have lapsed).

[]: Re: Which Eva series Dvd should I be buying?             Quote Post by NightEva » Thu Jun 02, 2016 6:25 pm Hey man sorry for the late reply, thanks for the information it cleared things up a lot for me, so basically I want to be looking for the non bootlegged platinum version for the DC's version of the show, I'll be getting right on it. I'm surprised there's no EoE that's been licensed properly since it's you know....EoE, but again I appreciate the information, hopefully we get the proper licensing for EoE cause I'd love to have that on Dvd too, it'd look real nice on my media storage cabinet right next to the little mermaid thanks Pwhodges.

[]: Re: Which Eva series Dvd should I be buying?             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sun Jun 05, 2016 3:10 am Khara released a super-packed Bluray of the series + films last year (it's on my desk & very sexy) so hopefully Funimation should finalize the rights to that release & get to bring it out here in the states within the near future. Can't imagine anyone else will beat Funimation at getting the rights & that they aren't salivating to sell the series because it would easily be their biggest seller of the year. I'd guess a new release should come out in the States sometime in 2017. A DVD would of course come out as well. Anywho, good luck on the DVD hunting. And NGE is a series worth double-dipping for.

[]: Re: Which Eva series Dvd should I be buying?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Jun 08, 2016 12:06 am The Japanese blu-rays are indeed sexy. And it seems obvious Funi would get their hands on them, but I don't count my chickens before they hatch. And NGE is certainly worth double-dipping. Heck, I've even triple-dipped a few episodes here and there just to get every single version available of certain episodes. (Narratively speaking, the Perfect Platinum or the Platinum versions from ADV are the ones to find on e-Bay. 4 episodes on that collection have two different versions of them, so you won't be left out on anything. If you want to get pedantic on how many frames are in each version of the episodes and what's inside them, you might want to supplement that collection with these discs . But you're really not missing anything by skipping out on those discs.)

[]: Re: Which Eva series Dvd should I be buying?             Quote Post by NightEva » Sat Jun 11, 2016 4:35 pm Damn now I'm reluctant to get the platinum if Japan has a version like that of the series, I'll put off buying the platinum for a while now cause i'd probably feel pretty stupid if they come out with that next year or so, and I don't see why Funi wouldn't do it all, hopefully soon, thanks for the information guys and that Bluray must be a real sexy piece x).
